Steps.Message:
Sent by Luke at 18:51 - We can now confirm that all power in the area should be back on. Our engineers had to unexpectedly turn off the power to 360 customers in the New Lodge Chase area to safely repair some overhead equipment which had come down.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Thanks for bearing with us during the power cut today, our staff will work to restore your power as quickly and as safely as possible. A lot of our customers have questions regarding power cuts, and though you can always reach our team, you can also find some helpful information and answers to some of our most frequently asked questions on our website here - https://UKPN.short.gy/FAQ - Bear in mind this will use your mobile data, so feel free to speak to us if you'd prefer. We'll be in touch again once we have more information from our staff.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Lee at 18:12 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Engineers have arrived on site in the London W14 area to investigate an underground cabling fault currently affecting several thousand properties. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off.

Using an average timescale for this type of issue we're looking to have you back on between 06:00-07:00, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We'll continue to update you with all of our progress.

Sent by Decasta at 05:34.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Decasta at 04:55 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground cabling fault that initially interrupted power to several thousand properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so some properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 06:00-07:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 04:23. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: Our engineers had to redirect power due to a fault on the electricity network.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Decasta at 06:03 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all affected customers in the London W14 area by diverting power around the underground cabling fault. We're pleased that we have been able to restore power sooner than we estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.
